key_themes: Steep valuation discount with high analyst price targets (~€93 avg vs current ~€68-69)
Ongoing €300m share buyback program (multiple 'transactions in own shares' in Mar-Apr 2026)
Cautious 2026 guidance (high single-digit adjusted EPS growth) after 2025 revenue/profit declines and EBITDA miss
Analyst consensus 'Outperform/Moderate Buy' (11-13 Buy ratings out of 13-18 analysts)
Long-term growth potential in taste & nutrition, wellness trends, despite near-term pressures
summary: Kerry Group (KYGA.L / KRZ.IR), the Irish food ingredients and taste & nutrition leader, reported softer 2025 results with revenue down ~2.5% and profit after tax down 10%, leading to missed targets, executive pay cuts, and a trimmed 2026 EPS growth outlook (6-10% constant currency, high single digits).[[1]](https://www.investing.com/news/earnings/kerry-group-cuts-2026-eps-outlook-misses-ebitda-launches-300-mln-buyback-4507946)[[2]](https://x.com/IrishTimesBiz/status/2034919280752369804) This drove the share price near its 52-week low (~€64-69 range vs prior highs near €100). However, the stock trades at a significant discount to intrinsic value and analyst targets (average ~€93, implying 35-38% upside), supported by a new €300m buyback, ongoing own-share transactions, a ~3% dividend yield, and mostly Buy/Outperform ratings from analysts including Goldman Sachs.[[3]](https://www.marketscreener.com/quote/stock/KERRY-GROUP-PLC-9676710/consensus/)[[4]](https://www.investing.com/news/analyst-ratings/goldman-sachs-initiates-kerry-group-stock-with-buy-rating-citing-growth-potential-93CH-4475378) Sentiment is cautiously positive among professionals who see compelling long-term value despite short-term headwinds.[[5]](https://x.com/businessposthq/status/2028810146214752504)
sources: 10+ web news/results from Yahoo Finance, Investing.com, Reuters, company RNS, Morningstar (Apr 2026 data), ~5-10 relevant X posts/mentions (mostly Irish business media shares; limited retail investor discussion), Analyst consensus reports (targets €92-95, Goldman Sachs Buy initiation)
